title: mqobsidian truth-export-index.v1
description: The single canonical entry point to mqobsidian's exported truth. It is the one surface a consumer reads FIRST: it enumerates every exported truth surface (views/status/inbox/... manifests and other canonical exports), states the schema+version each conforms to, where the materialized surface lives, and its freshness/drift. Consumers discover and version-check surfaces here, then resolve into the specific manifest — they never hardcode surface paths or assume a schema version. mqobsidian owns and is the sole producer of this index under the single-source-of-truth model (ROADMAP Delivery A/D; see DEC-002). This closes Delivery D's 'version exported truth surfaces' and 'expose freshness and drift markers'.

schema.description: Schema discriminator. Consumers must match this exact value before trusting the shape.

source.description: Producer identity that generated this index. mqobsidian is the sole producer.

generated_at.description: When the index itself was materialized. A consumer comparing this against now gets a top-level staleness read for the whole export.

surfaces.description: Every exported truth surface mqobsidian publishes. The array is the complete discovery set; a surface not listed here is not a canonical export.

key.description: Stable identifier a consumer requests a surface by (e.g. views, status, inbox). Decoupled from both path and schema id; renaming a key is a consumer-contract change.

schema.description: The versioned schema id this surface conforms to (e.g. status-manifest.v1). Carries the version explicitly so a consumer can refuse an unexpected major before parsing.

path.description: Vault-relative path to the materialized surface, resolved under the consumer's vault root (e.g. MQ_OBSIDIAN_DIR). Never absolute; never machine-specific.

generated_at.description: When this specific surface was last materialized. May be older than the index's generated_at; that gap is itself a freshness signal.

drift.description: True when this surface is known to lag its source evidence (a drift marker, Delivery D). Consumers may warn but must still read; absence asserts no known drift.

record_count.description: Optional count of records in the surface, for a cheap sanity/health read without fetching the surface.

description.description: Optional human-readable note about the surface for menus/tooling only; carries no resolution meaning.
